title: Roasted Squash with Crispy Grains & Cannellini Hummus
summary: This roasted squash with olive salsa, white bean hummus and crispy grains packs in plenty of fibre and over 10 plants per plate, making it a feast for you and your gut microbes.

method.description: If using a small butternut squash, halve it lengthways and remove the seeds. If using a large squash, halve it and remove the seeds then cut it into large chunks.
Get the squash on a baking tray and drizzle with olive oil and a pinch of salt. Add the unpeeled garlic cloves to the tray.

Roast for around 20-30 mins or until the squash is fork tender then remove the garlic cloves and sprinkle over the pre-cooked grains. Place back in the oven for another 10 mins or so, until the squash is completely tender and the grains are crispy.

Meanwhile, squeeze the flesh from the roasted garlic cloves into a blender and add the drained beans, tahini, juice of the lemon and a pinch of salt. Blitz with a couple of ice cubes until it’s completely smooth. Taste to check the seasoning and set aside.
Chop the olives and parsley together then add to a bowl with the olive oil and apple cider vinegar. Mix to combine and season to taste.
Assemble your plate with the hummus on the bottom, topped with the roasted squash, crispy grains, olive salsa and some sliced red chilli.

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
